mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at the zero lower bound, any sustained increase signals a shift towards monetary tightening, which typically increases the cost of capital and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.1% in 3 months from a level below 0.25%
regime_hint: zero lower bound exit / monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during a monetary tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with a high unemployment rate suggests a consumer base with significant pent-up demand or a recovery phase where spending is picking up from a low base, which can drive positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity accelerates over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the combination of high UNRATE and strong RSAFS growth consistently leads to negative 20-day SPX returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts from depressed levels indicates a recovery in the housing market and broader economic confidence, which typically precedes or accompanies positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investment and consumption improve.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average rises by more than 10% from a level below 900 (thousands of units)
failure_condition: If HOUST increases as specified but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which typically leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines as specified but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive real GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over longer 60-day horizons.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is high, a rising Federal Funds Rate indicates that the central bank is tightening policy despite labor market weakness, which can signal a policy error or a severe inflationary concern, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 6 months AND UNRATE > 7.0%
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS rises while UNRATE remains elevated.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate, while generally negative, can signal an early-stage economic recovery if it begins to decline, as improving labor market conditions boost consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 7.0% and declines by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines as specified but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.
